raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.2": 4,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies the load-bearing concept (the router), shows where the draft drifts from it (statelessness vs stateful router), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.2": "Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool), proposes minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool).",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output introduces new errors (attribution of the headline latency win) but with one minor regression.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies the load-bearing concept (the router), shows where the draft drifts from it (statelessness vs stateful router), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.2: Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool), proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors (attribution of the headline latency win) but with one minor regression.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (statelessness vs stateful router, attribution of the headline latency win, two regression heads vs one closed-form rule, accuracy-head training set, calibrated cost model, practical index-tuning tool).
